ops.title: Exclude test fixtures and vendored gitleaks config from secret scanning
ops.body: GitHub secret scanning flagged 17 'Google API Key' alerts on v0.19.0 commit e15f9fec — all false positives: 16 are upstream gitleaks v8.30.1's own gcp-api-key [[rules.allowlists]] literals (publicly-known example keys, vendored byte-verbatim in internal/secrets/gitleaks.toml per doc 1ab006d), 1 is the synthetic AIza-shaped fixture in internal/secrets/mask_test.go. Resolution: .github/secret_scanning.yml paths-ignore covers internal/secrets/gitleaks.toml and internal/secrets/*_test.go (intentional — a repo vendoring a secret-detection ruleset necessarily contains key-shaped strings), and the 17 alerts were dismissed via API as false_positive / used_in_tests. Do NOT defang the toml (byte-verbatim provenance) or the test fixture (regex must fire). Future rule bumps or new key-shaped test fixtures under internal/secrets/ are covered by the ignore; key-shaped strings elsewhere are NOT and should alert.
